commit.message:
Create PRISM - interactive light refraction art piece

Built interactive canvas piece where you drag a triangular glass prism 
to rotate it and watch white light split into 7-color rainbow spectrum.
Tap anywhere to send light rays that burst into spectrum particles.

Features:
- Draggable prism with rotation physics
- Continuous white/golden light beam from left
- 7-color refraction: violet → electric blue → teal → lime → gold → coral → hot pink
- Interactive light rays with particle bursts
- Full spectrum palette in balanced saturation
- Fade trails and glow effects
- Real-time angle/particle stats

Seed word: prism. Energy 0.59, valence 0.95 (animated quadrant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: fix Tesseract/Leptonica build failures on 3 targets

Three fixes for the remaining build-deps failures:

1. macOS x86_64: Tesseract 5.5.2 uses std::filesystem unconditionally,
   unavailable on our macOS 10.14 deployment target. Polyfill with
   ghc::filesystem v1.5.14 (header-only, MIT license) via sed-patch
   of baseapi.cpp and ccutil.cpp.

2. Windows (both targets): Tesseract links -lWs2_32 (mixed case) but
   MinGW sysroots have libws2_32.a (lowercase). Fix by removing the
   CLI executable build entirely — we only need the static library.

3. Windows (latent): Leptonica sets a versioned .dll suffix on MINGW
   even for static builds, producing libleptonica-1.87.0.dll instead
   of libleptonica.a. Guard with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS check.

Also remove redundant SED_I redefinition (already set in graphite2 section).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major overhaul: ATR SL, 1:1 partial, longs-only default, daily cap

- ATR(14) × 1.5 stop loss — adapts to gold volatility, not arbitrary pips
- Partial close: 50% at 1:1 (was 30% at 2:1) — locks profit faster
- Final TP: 2:1 (was 4:1) — much more achievable on M15
- Default sentiment: Longs only (was Both) — aligns with gold bull run
- Max 1 trade/day added — stops overtrading
- Max 3 trades/week (was 6) — forces selectivity
- Max 1 open position (was 2)

commit.message:
feat(output): add legacy decision context fallback parsing and extend new_audit constructor usage

Two related improvements to decision context handling in the audit/output
pipeline:

renderers.rs — Legacy decision context backward compatibility:
- Add parse_legacy_decision_context_object() that deserializes arbitrary
  JSON objects when the typed DecisionContext parse fails, enabling
  graceful rendering of older audit records that predate the structured
  DecisionContext schema
- Add legacy_decision_context_value() and
  legacy_decision_context_scalar_pairs() helpers for extracting
  human-readable key=value summaries from arbitrary JSON fields
- Refactor format_decision_context_summary() and
  format_decision_context_evidence() to try typed parse first, then fall
  back to legacy object parsing, so both old and new audit records render
  correctly
- Add two tests: one for legacy objects with explicit evidence arrays,
  one for plain scalar-only legacy objects

main.rs — Extend DecisionContext::new_audit constructor usage:
- Convert four more call sites (build_approval_decision_context,
  build_event_mutation_decision_context, build_ipc_rpc_decision_context,
  build_plan_audit_context) from verbose struct-literal initialization to
  the new_audit() constructor, continuing the refactor started in
  43a8ba0f; eliminates ~40 lines of boilerplate default field
  assignments per call site

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: wire full agentic automation into Sentinel ingestion pipeline

Every new intelligence event now triggers a 5-stage automation chain:

1. PATTERN DETECTION — keyword spike detection (26 tracked escalation
   keywords, 2h sliding window vs 7d baseline, z-score anomalies) +
   geographic convergence (3+ event types in same country within 24h)

2. WATCHLIST MATCHING — ALL events checked against ALL active watchlists
   (COUNTRY, ENTITY, KEYWORD, SUPPLIER, SECTOR, ROUTE), not just
   CRITICAL/HIGH. Users watching "Iran" or "OPEC" get matches regardless
   of severity.

3. KNOWLEDGE GRAPH — auto-extract persons, organizations, infrastructure
   from headlines/summaries and link to event graph. Builds relationship
   network automatically as events flow in.

4. AI REASONING — fire-and-forget DeepSeek R1 chain-of-thought analysis
   auto-triggered for CRITICAL/HIGH events. Results stored in
   ReasoningHistory with predicted outcomes for forecast accuracy tracking.

5. TRIAGE AGENT — existing 6-stage pipeline (screening → scoring →
   watchlist → briefing → case → notify) for high-severity events.

Additional automation:
- Ingest cron now runs GDELT news velocity enrichment for 10 priority
  countries after RSS ingestion completes
- Webhook broadcasts for keyword spikes + geographic convergences sent
  to ALL active webhook subscribers
- New /api/sentinel/digest endpoint — AI-generated daily intelligence
  digest summarizing top events, category breakdown, geographic hotspots,
  and strategic assessment. Delivered to all org admins.
- broadcastAlertToAll() function for system-level webhook delivery
- IngestionResult now includes full automation stats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): redesign UI with wallet-first flow and mailbox onboarding

- Replace always-visible tabs with a state machine: no-wallet →
  checking → no-tap → tx-pending → ready
- Remove session key mode entirely — wallet required to use the app
- Onboarding screen: after connecting wallet, check on-chain tap;
  if none found, show pending message count (registered via inbox auth)
  and an "Open Mailbox" button that calls
  sm-reservoir::create-tap-with-borrowed-liquidity
- Inbox shows message metadata only (from, amount, date, status);
  content stays encrypted — wallet ECIES decryption is out of scope
  until wallets add support
- Compose flow unchanged; always signs with wallet via SIP-018
- Status tab shows wallet identity + live channel state

commit.message:
feat(a2a): rewrite to Google A2A spec — JSON-RPC 2.0 transport, agent cards, task lifecycle

Replace custom REST-based A2A messaging with spec-compliant Google A2A protocol:

- JSON-RPC 2.0 transport (tasks/send, tasks/get, tasks/cancel, tasks/sendSubscribe)
- Agent card discovery at /.well-known/agent.json
- Task lifecycle: submitted → working → input-required → completed/canceled/failed
- Message/Part types (TextPart, FilePart, DataPart) with full roundtrip serialization
- SSE streaming via handle_sse() for real-time task updates
- Push notification configuration (set/get)
- Sardis payment skills as DataPart payloads within standard A2A framework
- Legacy types (A2AMessage, SardisAgentCard) re-exported for backward compatibility

116 tests passing (34 new spec + 26 rewritten + 56 legacy).

Replace the form-based workflow builder with a full pan/zoom canvas.
Nodes for all 7 step types (trigger, agent_task, approval, condition,
publish, delay, notify) render as styled cards with animated edges.
Clicking a node opens a right-side config panel; the toolbar provides
Add Step, Templates, Run, and Save actions. Run-history chips appear
bottom-left; node overlays show live run status.

- Install @xyflow/react; add React Flow dark-theme CSS overrides
- Extend WorkflowStep with position field and WorkflowNodeType union
- Shell gains canvas prop (overflow-hidden, no padding) for full-bleed layout
- stepsToNodes / stepsToEdges / nodesToSteps conversion utilities
- Three built-in templates: SEO→Blog Post, Product Launch, Promo Email

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add React Promtail logging with telemetry service (#497)

Implements client-side telemetry service for tracking events, page views, errors, and API dependencies.
The service batches telemetry events and sends them to a server endpoint for centralized logging.

Key features:
- TelemetryService for tracking page views, custom events, exceptions, and dependencies
- TelemetryBatcher for efficient batching and retrying of telemetry events
- SessionManager for managing user session IDs
- React hooks and providers for easy integration
- API client interceptors for automatic dependency tracking
- Comprehensive test coverage with mocks
- Error boundary integration for automatic exception tracking

This sets up the foundation for client-side observability to be sent to a Promtail/Loki logging stack.

feat: add unit tests + post-deploy smoke tests with auto-rollback to CI

- Unit test job (Vitest) runs before Docker build — blocks deploy on failure
- Post-deploy smoke tests hit the live Cloud Run URL via Playwright:
    homepage renders, blog shows articles, article SSR content, no 500s
- Auto-rollback: if smoke tests fail after a successful deploy, the
  previously deployed image is restored via gcloud run services update

  pointing Playwright directly at the live service instead

feat: add 4 review modes, UX improvements, output quality, and eval expansion (v0.4.0/v0.5.0)

perf (bottleneck ID). New commands: diff (git changes), help, history.
Flags: --verbose, --quiet, --codex-model, --gemini-model.

Output quality: confidence indicators (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), priority matrix,
follow-up tracking. Context injection auto-reads referenced files.

Eval framework: 6 new evals including adversarial injection resistance
and meta self-review. CLAUDE.md added with Serena MCP init instructions.

files keep BE/FE OOP constants in sync.
